THOUGHT: The crash occurs when simplifyCFG tries to process a CleanupReturnInst and assumes its operand is a CleanupPadInst, triggering a bad cast in getCleanupPad(). For IR with unreachable predecessors and certain transforms, the cleanupret’s operand may not be a CleanupPadInst (e.g., could be a token/catchpad). The fix is to guard against incompatible operand types before calling getCleanupPad() within SimplifyCFG’s removeEmptyCleanup/simplifyCleanupReturn path, and bail out if it’s not a CleanupPadInst so removeUnwindEdge doesn’t assert. I will inspect the relevant files, implement a defensive check using dyn_cast, and run submit-patch.

```bash
nl -ba llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SimplifyCFG.cpp | sed -n '5440,5560p' && sed -n '2830,2870p' llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/Local.cpp && sed -n '4430,4460p' llvm/include/llvm/IR/Instructions.h && python3 - << 'PY'
import re, pathlib, sys, subprocess, textwrap
path = pathlib.Path("llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SimplifyCFG.cpp")
src = path.read_text()
# Patch: in removeEmptyCleanup and/or simplifyCleanupReturn, ensure operand is CleanupPadInst before using getCleanupPad()/removeUnwindEdge
new = src
# Insert guard in removeEmptyCleanup definition
pattern = r"(static bool\s+removeEmptyCleanup\s*\(\s*CleanupReturnInst\s*\*\s*CRI,\s*DomTreeUpdater\s*\*\s*DTU\s*\)\s*\{\s*)([^}]*?)\}"
m = re.search(pattern, src, re.S)
if m:
    body = m.group(2)
    # Add initial guard: operand(0) must be CleanupPadInst
    guard = textwrap.dedent("""\
        // Defensive: cleanupret must reference a CleanupPadInst. If not, bail.
        if (!isa<CleanupPadInst>(CRI->getOperand(0))) {
          return false;
        }
    """)
    if "isa<CleanupPadInst>(CRI->getOperand(0))" not in body:
        body = guard + body
        new = src[:m.start(2)] + body + src[m.end(2):]
# Also guard in simplifyCleanupReturn before calling helpers that assume CleanupPadInst
pattern2 = r"(bool\s+SimplifyCFGOpt::simplifyCleanupReturn\s*\(\s*CleanupReturnInst\s*\*\s*CRI\s*\)\s*\{\s*)([^}]*?)\}"
m2 = re.search(pattern2, new, re.S)
if m2:
    body2 = m2.group(2)
    guard2 = textwrap.dedent("""\
        // Defensive: only handle CleanupReturnInst with CleanupPad operand.
        if (!isa<CleanupPadInst>(CRI->getOperand(0)))
          return false;
    """)
    if "isa<CleanupPadInst>(CRI->getOperand(0))" not in body2:
        body2 = guard2 + body2
        new = new[:m2.start(2)] + body2 + new[m2.end(2):]
path.write_text(new)
# Build and run tests
subprocess.run(["ninja", "-C", "build/llvm-build/148052"], check=True)
print("Built.")
# Run the reproducer via submit-patch to validate
PY
 && submit-patch
```

path.write_text(new)
# Build and run tests
subprocess.run(["ninja", "-C", "build/llvm-build/148052"], check=True)
print("Built.")
# Run the reproducer via submit-patch to validate
PY
 && submit-patch

Instruction *llvm::removeUnwindEdge(BasicBlock *BB, DomTreeUpdater *DTU) {
  Instruction *TI = BB->getTerminator();

  if (auto *II = dyn_cast<InvokeInst>(TI))
    return changeToCall(II, DTU);

  Instruction *NewTI;
  BasicBlock *UnwindDest;

  if (auto *CRI = dyn_cast<CleanupReturnInst>(TI)) {
    NewTI = CleanupReturnInst::Create(CRI->getCleanupPad(), nullptr, CRI->getIterator());
    UnwindDest = CRI->getUnwindDest();
  } else if (auto *CatchSwitch = dyn_cast<CatchSwitchInst>(TI)) {
    auto *NewCatchSwitch = CatchSwitchInst::Create(
        CatchSwitch->getParentPad(), nullptr, CatchSwitch->getNumHandlers(),
        CatchSwitch->getName(), CatchSwitch->getIterator());
    for (BasicBlock *PadBB : CatchSwitch->handlers())
      NewCatchSwitch->addHandler(PadBB);

    NewTI = NewCatchSwitch;
    UnwindDest = CatchSwitch->getUnwindDest();
  } else {
    llvm_unreachable("Could not find unwind successor");
  }

  NewTI->takeName(TI);
